title: Roundoff — comparing floats safely
problem: Add 0.1 + 0.2 and check whether the result equals 0.3.

trace.note: Same total, 0.30000000000000004 — still a hair off 0.3. The roundoff error has not gone away.
trace.checkpoint_prompt: total is 0.30000000000000004 — a hair off 0.3. After `close = abs(total - 0.3) < 0.0001` runs, will close be True or False? (Add a variable named close and predict it.)

trace.note: But this time we ask whether total is CLOSE to 0.3, within a small tolerance. The difference is tiny, far under 0.0001, so close is True.

result = 'close enough'. The fix for float comparison: never ==, check that the difference is small. Exactly the same numbers as the buggy version, opposite verdict — because the question changed from 'equal?' to 'close enough?'.
